title: brachychiton vitifolius
intro: Brachychiton vitifolius, also known as Illawarra flame tree or Illawarra flame, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The leaves and bark of the Brachychiton vitifolius plant are rich in tannins and have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant's bark has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bark can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused in hot water to utilize its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
